title: Is it appropriate for a husband to joke about taking a second wife?
stanceSummary: The Prophet (peace be upon him) never joked about taking a second wife, and doing so is not from the Sunnah. A woman has feelings, and it is not gentlemanly for a husband to provoke those feelings by joking about or dangling the prospect of a second wife. While polygamy itself may be permissible under appropriate circumstances, threatening, teasing, or treating it as humor causes genuine hurt and is unbecoming of a Muslim husband.
alternateQuestions: Is it wrong for a husband to joke about a second wife in Islam?
Did the Prophet joke about polygamy?
Is threatening your wife with a second marriage permissible?
How should a Muslim husband approach the topic of second marriage?
Is it sunnah to tease your wife about marrying another woman?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2025 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE) (starting at [33:10](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=1990s)), Yasir Qadhi addressed the common practice of husbands joking about second wives. His position:

- **The Prophet (peace be upon him) never joked about a second wife** — this is not from the Sunnah.
- A woman has feelings, and it is **not gentlemanly** to provoke those feelings for no reason.
- If one wants to follow the Sunnah in this regard, they should do it **the proper way**, not by joking, dangling, or threatening.
- There is **genuine hurt** felt by wives in the community, and this behavior should stop.

---

## Full Lecture Transcript (Cleaned)

### The Question — [33:10](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=1990s)

Sisters are asking — brothers, pay attention. In every single Q&A, you get questions about second marriages. Just wondering — why do the men find this so amusing when none of their wives find it amusing? Why are you laughing? I'm literally telling you the sisters are complaining that you're laughing.

One of the sisters is saying: in every Q&A there is a question about second wives. Do you guys find it funny? Because none of them find it funny. And believe it or not, I saw this question and five questions down, there's a question about a second wife — literally.

### The Prophet's Example — [33:50](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mrY7Yqw7eSE&t=2030s)

I've said this before in the youth gathering, and I'll say it to the adults gathering as well. SubhanAllah — **the Prophet (peace be upon him) never joked about a second wife**. This is not of the Sunnah to do so. I'm not saying getting married is not sunnah — I'm saying **he never joked about a second wife**, because a woman has feelings, and it is not a part of the gentleman and the husband to provoke those feelings for no reason.

If you want to follow the Sunnah of the Prophet (peace be upon him) in this aspect — in another country where it was permissible to do so — do it in the Sunnah way. But **joking and dangling it in front of your first wife and threatening and thinking it's funny — that's definitely not Sunnah and it's not gentlemanly to do**. Really, it's not.

I actually wanted to quote the sister's expression to you. This is literally coming from one of the sisters in our community, and it's a valid point: if a legitimate question happens, I understand, but there is a **genuine sense of hurt** in this regard, and I don't see this as being a part of what we should be doing. So, a valid point, insha'Allah.
